semantic.role: GitLab CI integration template for automating ArchSpine sync and architecture check during merge requests.
responsibilities: Providing a ready-to-use `.gitlab-ci.yml` example for a `spine-check` job
Documenting prerequisites: `spine init`, environment variables (LLM_API_KEY, GITLAB_TOKEN)
Explaining usage via direct file copy or remote include
Detailing the CI script: `spine sync --no-docs`, `spine check`, MR comment posting on failure
Defining artifact retention for failure output
outOfScope: Non-GitLab CI platforms (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, etc.)
Manual execution of `spine sync` or `spine check` outside CI

English.purpose: Provide a plug-and-play GitLab CI job that runs 'spine sync' and 'spine check' on every merge request, and automatically posts a detailed comment to the MR when architecture violations are detected.
English.context_and_audience: Targeted at development teams using GitLab CI who have already initialized ArchSpine in their repository and want to enforce architectural rules automatically as part of their merge request workflow.
key_takeaways: Automates architecture compliance as a CI step during merge requests.
Requires two GitLab CI/CD variables: LLM_API_KEY and GITLAB_TOKEN.
Posts the full `spine check` output as an MR comment on failure.
Can be used as a standalone file or included remotely into an existing `.gitlab-ci.yml`.
